instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How ULIP Benefits Individuals Who Are Starting a Family?
Starting a family is a whole new set of responsibilities. Besides the obvious joy of welcoming your bundle of joy, you may also need to make financial provisions for your family’s newest and youngest member. Whether it is the costs associated with childbirth or the expenses of raising the child, the financial responsibilities increase considerably. That is why individuals starting a family should make provisions for the expected increase in expenses.
A unit-linked life insurance policy (ULIP) can be one of the provisions that individuals starting a family can consider. A ULIP is an insurance -cum- investment plan that helps you build up a corpus for your increased financial goals. Moreover, with the insurance coverage that the ULIP plan provides, you can also secure your family's financial future when you are not around. Thus, ULIPs give dual benefits and can be the preferred financial planning tool.
How a ULIP benefits individuals starting a family?
Two distinct ways individuals starting a family can benefit from a ULIP. These are as follows –
Creation of a corpus for new financial goals:
Being market-linked investment-oriented plans, ULIPs can allow you to save towards the new financial goals that the new family member may bring. You can save to buy a new car or move into a new home. Similarly, you can start saving for your child’s education and other future needs. With the market-linked returns that ULIPs provide, you can create a suitable corpus for your financial goals. You can use the ULIP calculator to estimate the market linked returns you can earn to plan for your goals accordingly.
ULIPs based Child Insurance Plans to secure your child’s financial future:
ULIPs based Child Insurance Plans are plans designed specifically to secure your child’s financial future even when you are not around. Depending from Insurer to Insurer, some  ULIPs usually come with a premium waiver benefit that waives off the premium in case of an unfortunate event of death of the policyholder. You can also opt for this benefit as an add-on rider by paying additional nominal premium. This helps the plan to, runs undisturbed till maturity and pays a maturity benefit to the surviving child. This benefit may give your child the corpus needed for higher education, marriage, or any of their financial needs. Thus, with a ULIP based Child insurance plan, you can safeguard your child’s financial future. A ULIP calculator can be used to check if the proposed market linked returns are aligned with your financial goal.
Other ULIP benefits to consider
While the aforementioned benefits make ULIPs the preferable solution while planning for your family’s finances, there are other benefits too that you may enjoy when you invest in the plan. Some of the benefits are as follows –
Partial withdrawals in emergencies:
Emergencies might strike unannounced, and in such cases, you might need additional funds to tackle the adversity that you face. The ULIP may come to your rescue in such situations. The plan allows partial withdrawals after the completion of the lock-in period of five years. So, in an emergency, you have an option to withdraw from your fund value and use the funds to meet your financial needs.
The flexibility of managing your investment:
ULIPs give you the flexibility of managing your investment. You can change between the investment funds through the fund switching facility, which is tax-free subject to satisfaction of certain conditions. So, if the market dynamics change or your investment strategy changes, you have an option to switch funds, however, this is subject to policy terms & conditions.
You can also pay additional premiums towards the plan to enhance your investments. This facility is provided through top-up premiums that enhance the fund value and the sum assured. This option can be opted subject to policy terms & conditions.
The premium redirection facility is also available that allows you to redirect your future premiums to another fund than what you selected originally. Like switching, this facility comes in handy with the market dynamics or your strategy change.
A range of funds to suit every investor:
ULIPs offer a multitude of funds so that you can invest per your risk appetite. You can choose from a range of equity funds, debt funds, and even hybrid funds that combine both equity and debt.
Pick one or multiple funds and you can even consider switching between them so that you manage your investments as per market dynamics.
Tax benefits on investments and returns¹:
In addition to the above benefits, you also get tax benefits on ULIPs. The premiums you pay qualify as a deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961 subject to provisions stated therein. You can claim a deduction of up to Rs.1.5 lakhs on premiums up to 10% of the death sum assured.
ULIPs with premiums exceeding Rs.2,50,000 will not qualify for exemption under section 10 (10D) of the Income Tax Act.
Switching and partial withdrawals are tax-free.
The death benefit attracts no taxes so that your family can have sufficient corpus in your absence.
Even the maturity benefit enjoys tax exemption under Section 10(10D) if the annual aggregate premium on the policies issued on or after 1st February 2021 is less than or equal to Rs.2.5 lakhs, subject to satisfaction of conditions mentioned in the Income Tax Act, 1961.
So, with ULIPs, you can plan your taxes and save tax on investments as well as on the returns.
In case of high-value ULIP, the tax treatment will be governed basis whether such policy qualifies to be unit of an ‘equity-oriented fund’.  If the fund invests minimum 65% of the total proceeds in equity shares of listed domestic company or 90% of total proceeds in units of another listed fund, which in-turn invest 90% of proceeds in equity shares of listed domestic company, in such a case it shall be treated as equity-oriented fund.  Such investment thresholds are required to be satisfied throughout the term of such insurance policy.
Thus, if the above investment thresholds are satisfied, the high-value ULIP shall be treated as unit of equity-oriented fund.  Else, ULIP shall be treated as unit of debt-oriented fund.  The classification of capital gains between long-term and short-term and the applicable tax rate shall be determined as follows as per the Income Tax Act, 1961.
If you are thinking of  starting your family, you may consider investing in a ULIP that would help in taking care of your finances and your family’s financial future.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.
Ref:
1https://www.taxmann.com/post/blog/taxation-of-unit-linked-insurance-plans-ulips-the-complete-picture-is-out/#3333
